findings.problem: The chapter's biggest movements are still preparatory rather than fully consequential on the page. School-wide revolt and the eve-of-battle turn are framed as pressure around Gavin's work, so the installment reads like strong setup for chapter 17 instead of a chapter that lands its own irreversible ordeal.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild one of the background-pressure blocks at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:21, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:149, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:167 into a present-time collision that changes Gavin's available options before the chapter ends. Keep the broken-wrist reveal, but give the late chapter a concrete turn: an interruption he refuses, a damaged workspace, a lost route, an exposed secret, or another immediate cost that leaves him materially worse off than he was at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:117.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material replacing the late chapter around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:149 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:175 leaves Gavin with a concrete new condition by the end of the chapter that is visible on the page and not merely anticipated. Fail if the chapter still closes with Gavin essentially continuing the same work under the same conditions, only more aware that battle is coming.

findings.problem: The chapter keeps its promised human rupture at observational distance. It names revolt, hiding students, rumors, and incoming fighters, but those beats arrive mainly as overview and substrate commentary rather than embodied social or physical consequence. In a chapter whose texture is meant to be hot and unavoidable, that summary-buffer reads as sanitization drift: scene pressure is reported instead of fully present.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:11, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:153, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:167, trade some report language for one rendered aftermath or interruption with sensory residue still on the page: a student in shock, a failed Carrow command landing on actual bodies, scorched stone still smoking, triage supplies being used, dust shaking loose into the sub-collection, or another concrete cost Gavin has to register in-scene. Gavin can stay emotionally alien; the prose cannot stay this buffered.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least one revised passage anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:9 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:153 shows a specific human cost or physical disturbance unfolding in-scene and leaving sensory residue after the moment. Fail if those sections still mostly summarize categories of unrest or approaching conflict without an embodied consequence the reader can point to.

findings.problem: Two explanatory templates dominate the chapter: repeated 'the way you...' analogic similes and repeated 'Not X ...' contrast openings. Used once or twice, each would sharpen Gavin's perception; used this often, they become audible scaffolding and flatten the prose into an autopilot rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the repeated template clusters at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:17, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:37, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:75, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:145, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:155, with attention also to nearby repeats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:35, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:119. Keep only the instances whose comparison does irreplaceable work; convert the others into direct sensory statement, a different sentence architecture, or a less pre-signaled turn of thought.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter contains no more than two 'the way you...' similes and no more than two paragraph-opening 'Not X ...' contrast turns, with each remaining instance doing distinct work in a distinct context. Fail if those templates are still the chapter's dominant explanatory rhythm across the cited passages.

summary: FAIL on award, craft, and prose. Gavin's voice and the limited dialogue stay style-bible consistent, with natural contraction use and no clear tense or continuity violations, but the chapter remains too setup-shaped for shortlist-level consequence: the human rupture is buffered by summary, and repeated sentence templates flatten otherwise strong material.
